title: The Three Types of Machine Learning
concept: Machine learning splits into three big approaches: supervised learning (labeled examples with an answer key), unsupervised learning (finding patterns with no labels), and reinforcement learning (learning from rewards and mistakes).

exercises.prompt: Recap: What makes machine learning different from traditional rule-based programming?

options.text: A human writes out every exact rule ahead of time
The computer finds patterns in examples instead of following hand-written rules
It doesn't use any data at all

exercises.explain: Right! Machine learning finds patterns in data instead of following rules a human typed out.

cards.title: Three Different Ways to Learn
cards.body: Every Saturday, Zara does three totally different kinds of "learning." She does homework her teacher grades. She sorts her overflowing sock drawer with no one's help. And she practices skateboard tricks by wiping out, again and again, until she doesn't. Machine learning works the exact same three ways.

cards.title: The three types of machine learning
cards.body: Supervised learning learns from labeled examples that already have the correct answer attached. Unsupervised learning finds patterns in data that has no labels at all. Reinforcement learning learns by taking actions and getting rewards or penalties.

cards.title: A teacher, a sock drawer, and a bike
cards.body: Supervised learning is like a teacher grading your homework — every answer gets checked against an answer key. Unsupervised learning is like sorting your own sock drawer — nobody labels the socks, you just group the ones that look alike. Reinforcement learning is like learning to ride a bike — you wobble, you fall, you adjust, and eventually you stop falling.

cards.title: Spotting each type in the wild
cards.body: Supervised: a spam filter trained on emails already marked "spam" or "not spam." Unsupervised: a store grouping shoppers into clusters it discovers on its own, with no pre-made categories. Reinforcement: a game-playing program that earns points for good moves and loses points for bad ones.

cards.title: It's not one giant blob called "AI"
cards.body: People often lump all machine learning into one mysterious blob. In reality, there are distinct approaches built for different jobs — whether you have an answer key, you don't, or you only find out how you did after acting.

cards.title: Labeled or not?
cards.body: The biggest fork in the road between two of the three types:

left.text:
Has an answer key
Learns from labeled examples

right.text:
No answer key
Finds its own groupings

exercises.prompt: Which type of machine learning learns from data that already has the correct answers attached?
exercises.explain: Right! Supervised learning trains on labeled examples that include the correct answer.

exercises.prompt: True or false: Unsupervised learning is given a labeled answer key to check its guesses against.
exercises.explain: False! Unsupervised learning gets no labels at all — it has to find patterns on its own.

exercises.sentence: Reinforcement learning learns by taking actions and receiving ___ or penalties.
exercises.explain: Reinforcement learning is driven by rewards and penalties, not labeled answers.

exercises.prompt: Which scenario best describes reinforcement learning?

options.text: A program that tries different chess moves, earning points for wins and losing points for bad moves
A program trained on 10,000 photos labeled "cat" or "not cat"
A program that groups customers into clusters with no predefined categories

exercises.explain: Exactly! Trying actions and earning points over time is the trial-and-error loop of reinforcement learning.

exercises.prompt: A robot vacuum bumps into furniture and gets a small penalty each time, and gets a reward each time it cleans a new patch of floor without bumping anything. Over many cleaning runs, it gets better at avoiding furniture. Which type of machine learning is this?

options.text: None of the above — this is rule-based programming

exercises.explain: Exactly! The vacuum takes actions, gets rewards and penalties, and improves through trial and error — that's reinforcement learning.
